food_name: Shake Shack Shack for Two Meal
food_subgroup: Burger meal set — beef patty burgers with sides and beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a multi-item burger meal set. FOODON_00002383 (hamburger) is used as the closest single-item anchor. The full set includes 2 burgers, 2 sides, and 2 drinks.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Hamburger) is the closest defensible anchor. No composite meal-set code exists in FoodEx2 for a 2-burger-2-side-2-drink bundle.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for a commercial burger meal set. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 1 meal set for two persons (2 burgers, 2 sides, 2 drinks)
serving.natural_language_portion: One Shack for Two set comprises 2 Shake Shack burgers (approximately 200 g each), 2 portions of crinkle-cut fries (approximately 170 g each), and 2 beverages (approximately 230 g each), totalling approximately 1,200 g for the full set or 600 g per person.

nutrient_source.title: Shake Shack Nutritional Information — ShackBurger, Crinkle-Cut Fries, and Shakes; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Beef patty, bun, fries, milkshake composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods); Shake Shack published nutrition data (used as secondary corroboration for branded product)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The Shack for Two is a composite meal set. Per-100g values are estimated by constructing a weighted composite of the typical Shake Shack ShackBurger (beef patty ~113g, brioche bun ~60g, lettuce/tomato/sauce ~30g), crinkle-cut fries (~170g per portion), and a vanilla milkshake (~230g per portion), scaled to a total set weight of approximately 1,200g. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries used: 'Fast foods, hamburger; double, large patty; with condiments and vegetables' (FDC ID 173965), 'Fast foods, potato, french fried' (FDC ID 172931), and 'Milk shakes, thick vanilla' (FDC ID 170893). Values represent a blended per-100g average across the full set. Shake Shack's own published nutrition panel was reviewed as a secondary corroboration source but is not the primary evidence source per Veritas source policy.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white bread and refined starch fast food composite analogy; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Shake Shack Shack for Two meal set. GI estimated at 66 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate sources in the set: white-flour brioche bun (GI ~70, analogous to white bread GI 75 per University of Sydney GI Database), crinkle-cut french fries (GI ~63 per University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson 2021), and vanilla milkshake (GI ~39 per University of Sydney GI Database). A weighted average across the carbohydrate contribution of each component yields an estimated composite GI of approximately 66. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 302.4g carbohydrate in 1,200g set) / 100 ≈ 39.5 per full set for two persons (approximately 19.8 per person).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~310 mg per 100g; approximately 960 mg per person serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal set, as a single-person portion provides approximately 40–48% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg, and should request reduced-sodium modifications where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load and medium-to-high glycemic index (~66) with a high glycemic load per serving (~20 per person)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this meal set as an occasional indulgence only, as the combination of refined-flour buns, fried potato sides, and a sweetened milkshake produces a high glycemic load (~20 per person) that is likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~3.8g per 100g), trans fat (~0.2g per 100g), sodium, and cholesterol content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this meal set only rarely, as the saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium levels across the full set substantially exceed recommended single-meal limits for cardiovascular risk management per AHA dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Shake Shack 'Shack for Two' is a sharing meal set available at Shake Shack restaurants in Singapore, including the 89 Neil Road and One Fullerton outlets, and listed on field survey data. The set comprises the customer's choice of 2 Shake Shack burgers, 2 sides (typically crinkle-cut fries), and 2 beverages (typically milkshakes or soft drinks), making it a complete meal for two persons. The flagship ShackBurger features a 100% Angus beef patty cooked to order on a flat-top grill, served on a soft potato bun with ShackSauce (a proprietary mayonnaise-based sauce), American cheese, crisp lettuce, and ripe tomato. The crinkle-cut fries are seasoned and fried to a crispy exterior with a fluffy interior. Milkshakes, if selected, are thick, creamy, and available in flavours such as vanilla, chocolate, and strawberry. Nutritionally, the full set is energy-dense (approximately 218 kcal per 100g; approximately 2,600 kcal for the full set), high in refined carbohydrates, saturated fat, and sodium, and carries a medium glycemic index of approximately 66 with a high glycemic load per person (~20). It is not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The set is priced at SGD 42.40 on field survey data and represents a premium fast-casual American burger dining experience in the Singapore market.

confidence_reason: No direct per-100g nutritional data exists for the Shake Shack Shack for Two composite meal set in any approved primary source. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fast-food hamburger, french fries, and vanilla milkshake, weighted by approximate component masses. The GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for the dominant carbohydrate components. Confidence is medium rather than low because the USDA SR Legacy sources are high-quality and the component-based estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. The Shack for Two is a composite meal set, not a single food item. Per-100g values represent a blended average across the full set (estimated total weight ~1,200g: 2 × ~200g burger, 2 × ~170g fries, 2 × ~230g beverage). 2. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported across the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for this estimation. 3. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported in the USDA SR Legacy fast-food composite entries used. 4. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA SR Legacy sources used for this composite estimation. 5. GI is an analogous estimate (GI ~66, medium) derived from University of Sydney GI Database values for white-flour bun (~70), crinkle-cut fries (~63), and vanilla milkshake (~39), weighted by carbohydrate contribution. 6. The meal set is not halal-certified at Shake Shack Singapore outlets as of the knowledge cutoff. 7. Allergens include gluten (bun), dairy (cheese, milkshake, ShackSauce), egg (ShackSauce), and soy (bun). 8. Price is SGD 42.40 as listed on field survey data. 9. Multilingual names reflect the Singapore market context (Western/American cuisine served in Singapore); Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi names are provided per G6 Singaporean-market multilingual rule applied to Singapore-served dishes.
